provenance.source_corpus: Restoration of belowground fungal communities in reclaimed landscapes of the Canadian boreal forest

nodes.name: Disturbances that remove or disrupt the organic layer of soils

source_spans.text: Disturbances that remove or disrupt the organic layer of soils substantially affect the composition of ectomycorrhizal fungal communities.
Disturbances that remove or disrupt the organic layer of soils substantially affect the composition of ectomycorrhizal fungal communities.
Shifts in the community composition of ectomycorrhizal fungi were driven to a greater extent by disturbance severity than either tree species planted or soil placement.
Ectomycorrhizal fungal OTU richness varied across host seedlings differing in age... with EcM fungal OTU richness highest on 4-year-old seedlings compared to 1- and 2-year-old seedlings
EcM fungal community composition differed among the three host species
EcM fungal community composition differed among FFM, peat, and subsoil on 4-year-old seedlings
Ectomycorrhizal fungal OTU richness varied across host seedlings differing in age
EcM fungal community composition differed among the three host species
Ectomycorrhizal fungal OTU richness varied by disturbance severity... lowest where trees and forest floor had been removed
